title: podman in rootless mode will only work with cgroupfs at this point.
body:
If user does not pass in cgroup manager and running in rootless mode,
then we need to force the cgroupfs support until/unless we get support
for rootless systemd support.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
@baude @mheon @giuseppe PTAL

I am not sure this is the correct long term fix, but I am adding this patch to the release so that we can get it out and podman will work as non root.

LGTM

I restarted the Jenkins job that failed due to the flaky network.

LGTM.
@rh-atomic-bot r+

from the CI errors: "container create failed: systemd cgroup flag passed, but systemd support for managing cgroups is not available" seems to happen also in the not rootless case

Move to containers/libpod seems to have busted our CI even more than it was previously.
@mheon @jlebon is working on this, should be fixed this afternoon.
Oh right, I don't have access to this repo. Can someone with push access do an `@rh-atomic-bot r=mheon`?
@jlebon I wouldn't be surprised if this one fails due to actual test failures
